definition: Purchase of existing LP interests in private funds from investors seeking liquidity or portfolio rebalancing.
growth_driver: LPs need liquidity, denominator effects, portfolio cleanup, and exit delays.
return_driver: Discount capture, mature asset distributions, manager selection.
key_risk: NAV quality, unfunded commitments, weak underlying funds.
neo_relevance: Useful for diversified global access, but hard to source directly at scale.

definition: A sponsor moves one or more assets into a new vehicle, allowing existing LPs to sell or roll.
growth_driver: Exit markets are slow and GPs want to hold strong assets longer.
return_driver: Asset-level growth, aligned sponsor ownership, extended hold period.
key_risk: Conflicts, valuation fairness, weak exit path.
neo_relevance: Attractive if Neo can access high-quality sponsor-led deals with strong governance.

definition: GP-led transaction centered on one portfolio company.
growth_driver: Sponsors want more time with trophy assets.
return_driver: Deep underwriting of one strong company.
neo_relevance: Relevant for co-investment club clients, but requires serious asset-level diligence.

definition: GP-led vehicle with several assets from an older fund.
growth_driver: GPs need liquidity solutions for older funds.
key_risk: Selection bias and mixed asset quality.
neo_relevance: More diversified than single-asset CVs, but still needs conflict checks.

definition: Structured capital solution that provides liquidity against private market portfolios.
growth_driver: Investors want liquidity without selling at steep discounts.
return_driver: Preferred return, downside protection, collateral coverage.
neo_relevance: Potentially attractive for Indian family-office liquidity needs.

definition: Secondary transactions involving private credit fund interests or loan portfolios.
growth_driver: Private credit AUM has grown rapidly and LPs need liquidity.
key_risk: Credit losses, opaque marks, liquidity mismatch.
neo_relevance: Worth tracking, especially for yield-seeking clients.

definition: Secondary purchases of infrastructure fund stakes or infrastructure assets.
growth_driver: Infra AUM growth, long fund lives, and liquidity demand.
return_driver: Stable cash flows, inflation linkage, discount capture.
neo_relevance: Potential fit for conservative family-office allocations.

definition: Secondaries exposure packaged for HNI and private wealth channels.
growth_driver: Wealth clients want private markets access with lower J-curve and more mature portfolios.
return_driver: Diversified secondaries exposure, faster distributions, manager access.
neo_relevance: Highly relevant. This is where Neo can differentiate through education, access, and reporting.
